metadata.summary: Rolling market-scanning cadence with model selection, recommended subagents, and the agentic workflow pattern for continuous competitor and pricing monitoring.

content.title: Continuous Discovery
content.summary: Continuous discovery is the practice of running weekly market-scanning and competitor-monitoring loops instead of one-shot research sprints. Rather than treating market intelligence as a project-start deliverable, it maintains a living picture of competitors, pricing, new entrants, and category signals on a daily/weekly/bi-weekly cadence using cheap models for data collection and Opus only for synthesis.

why.p: Markets in fast-moving categories (AI tooling, dev tools, PLG SaaS) shift faster than quarterly research cycles. Competitors ship weekly, pricing A/B-tests daily, and new entrants arrive via YC batches and ProductHunt. A rolling scan catches these moves before they show up in churn, while a severity rubric (0-5) filters cosmetic noise from genuine strategic events.

list.item: Live category where competitors ship weekly and a 6-month-old market map is already wrong.
Pricing-sensitive segments where competitor packaging changes erode conversion within days.
GTM teams needing a weekly "what changed" digest without burning a senior analyst.
Funded categories where new entrants (YC batches, ProductHunt launches) appear faster than a quarterly TAM refresh catches.
Geo expansion plays where local competitors and regulatory shifts move the SOM monthly.
Post-launch defense: detecting when a fast-follower clones your wedge before churn shows up.
Solopreneur stacks operating like a one-person research firm — the only way to keep a monitoring matrix alive is via cron + agents.
Pre-PMF zero-to-one with no defined competitive set — run competitor-analysis once first.
Slow-moving regulated categories (medtech, defense, classical banking) where half-life is years.
Compliance-bound enterprise sales with 12-18 month cycles — quarterly snapshots beat noisy weekly diffs.
Single-customer custom-software work — no "market" to scan.
When the team will not act on signals — unread scans become a research graveyard that burns tokens.
Crisis mode (active outage, churn cliff) — pause scan, focus on incident, resume after stabilization.

rule.statement: Run cheap models (haiku) for daily collection, sonnet for structured weekly analysis, and opus only for bi-weekly synthesis and quarterly TAM refresh.
rule.rationale: Token cost is the primary constraint on continuous scanning; haiku handles fetch/diff, opus handles strategic synthesis — mixing them inverts the cost-to-signal ratio.

list.item: Daily: market-pulse (haiku) → market-pulse-<date>.md
Daily: competitor-changelog-watcher (haiku) → competitor-changes.md (append-only)
Daily: pricing-watcher (haiku) → pricing-snapshots/<date>/
Weekly: review-mining (sonnet) → review-themes.md (G2/Capterra/Reddit)
Weekly: funding-news-watcher (haiku) → funding-events.md
Weekly: ad-creative-watcher (sonnet) → ad-library/<vendor>/<date>/
Weekly: seo-serp-watcher (haiku) → serp-deltas.md
Bi-weekly: market-synthesizer (opus) → market-map.md, threat-board.md
Monthly: market-strategist (opus) → market-memo.md, kill/double-down list
Quarterly: tam-refresh (opus) → tam-sam-som.md (versioned)

rule.statement: All outputs must live under .aidocs/market-intel/. The synthesizer reads weekly diffs and updates versioned market-map.md + threat-board.md; it never rewrites history.
rule.rationale: Versioned outputs let the strategist diff Q-over-Q rather than re-derive each time; append-only logs prevent concurrency overwrites from parallel haiku agents.

list.item: market-pulse (haiku, daily): Tavily/SerpAPI top-of-day, Google News RSS, HN front page → 10-bullet digest tagged by competitor
competitor-changelog-watcher (haiku, daily): public changelog URLs, RSS, GitHub releases, Twitter/X handles → per-competitor diff with severity 1-5
pricing-watcher (haiku, daily): pricing pages logged-out + 2-3 geos → diff vs prior snapshot, $-delta, packaging-delta
review-mining (sonnet, weekly): G2/Capterra/TrustRadius/Reddit/AppStore → theme cluster (gain/pain/switch reason), N≥5 rule
funding-news-watcher (haiku, weekly): Crunchbase API, PitchBook RSS, TechCrunch tag feeds → funding event list with stage/amount/investors
ad-creative-watcher (sonnet, weekly): Meta Ad Library API, TikTok Creative Center, LinkedIn Ad Library → active creatives + messaging hooks
seo-serp-watcher (haiku, weekly): Ahrefs/SEMrush API, tracked keywords → rank deltas, new pages, BOFU keyword shifts
marketplace-watcher (haiku, weekly): ProductHunt API, AppSumo RSS, YC Launch RSS → new entrants with category match score
market-synthesizer (opus, bi-weekly): all above + last market-map.md → updated map, threat board, opportunity list
market-strategist (opus, monthly): all synth outputs + sales-CRM win/loss → strategic memo: kill/double-down/watch buckets
tam-refresh (opus, quarterly): market reports, public filings, customer counts → versioned TAM/SAM/SOM with delta

section.name: Prompt Pattern

rule.statement: Tag every observation with source_url, fetched_at, competitor_id, signal_type (changelog|pricing|review|funding|ad|serp|launch), severity (1-5), confidence (0-1). Reject any change missing source_url + raw-content hash.
rule.rationale: Without the full triple (source_url + fetched_at + raw_hash), fabricated pricing and changelog entries cannot be detected or invalidated retroactively.

section.reference: Claude Agent SDK watcher implementing the fetch + diff + hash + append pattern
Competitor registry schema with all required fields per competitor
Shell validator enforcing the source_url + fetched_at + raw_hash triple

rule.statement: Apply severity 0-5 to every signal: 5 = pricing/packaging move; 4 = positioning shift / new ICP; 3 = new feature in core wedge; 2 = peripheral feature; 1 = blog/PR; 0 = cosmetic.
rule.rationale: Without a severity rubric, every blog post registers as "competitor activity" and buries severity-5 pricing moves in noise.

rule.statement: Every severity-4+ signal must produce a "respond / monitor / ignore" decision in the next weekly cycle, written into threat-board.md.
rule.rationale: A continuously scanned market that nobody acts on becomes a research graveyard and burns tokens without strategic value.

rule.statement: Require a quarterly source-health audit. When a source goes 30 days without change or returns errors 3 consecutive runs, escalate to human audit and log in dead-source.md.
rule.rationale: SaaS competitors disable RSS, hide changelogs behind login, and move to gated Slack — the scan rots silently without detection.

rule.statement: Capture pricing from at least 2 geos + logged-out + incognito. Require ≥2 geo confirmations before flagging a price change.
rule.rationale: Pricing pages A/B-test by region and cookie — single-shot scrapes give a misleading reading.

rule.statement: Diff content blocks, not raw DOM. Use readability/trafilatura extraction before diffing to strip nav, footer, and cookie banners.
rule.rationale: Boilerplate generates 90% of false-positive diffs; full-DOM diff makes cosmetic CSS changes register as full-page changes.

rule.statement: Pin one strategic Outcome before any scan. Without it, agents track 40 competitors instead of the 5 that move the SOM.
rule.rationale: Agents cheerfully expand scope unless capped by an explicit outcome anchor.

rule.statement: Maintain a versioned competitor-registry.json. All additions/removals via PR — never silent edits — to tie signals to stable competitor_id values.
rule.rationale: Signal correlation across cadences requires stable IDs; silent registry edits break the audit trail.

antipattern.description: LLM hallucinating competitor pricing or changelog entries when scrapes fail, reporting "$49 → $99 price hike" without a source.
antipattern.reason: Without source_url + fetched_at + raw_hash triple enforced at schema level, fabricated diffs are indistinguishable from real ones. Enforce the triple and reject completions missing any field.

antipattern.description: Daily haiku watchers overwriting shared files instead of appending.
antipattern.reason: Concurrent agents writing to the same file produce corrupt outputs. Use atomic JSONL append + lock file, never Write on shared logs.

antipattern.description: Bi-weekly synthesis with full week's signals exceeding context window.
antipattern.reason: Pre-summarize per-day with haiku, feed only summaries to opus for synthesis — never pass raw haiku dumps directly to the synthesizer.

antipattern.description: Review-mining agent asserting "competitor X has churn" from headlines rather than verbatim quotes.
antipattern.reason: Require quote-level evidence (verbatim text + source URL) before any sentiment claim — summary paraphrases hallucinate sentiment direction.

antipattern.description: "New entrant" agent over-triggering on rebrands of existing players.
antipattern.reason: Match on domain + founders + funding history, not just name. A rebrand of a known competitor is not a new entrant.

list.item: Pin one strategic Outcome (e.g. "defend SMB SOM in EU") before any scan; without it agents track 40 competitors instead of the 5 that matter.
Maintain a versioned competitor-registry.json — additions/removals via PR, never silent edits; ties signals to a stable competitor_id.
Capture pricing from at least 2 geos + logged-out + incognito; otherwise A/B tests look like price changes.
Hash raw HTML/PDF of every signal source; future re-checks against the hash catch silent CMS edits and Wayback discrepancies.
Diff content blocks, not full DOM — boilerplate (nav, footer, cookie banners) generates 90% of false-positive diffs.
Severity rubric is mandatory: 5 = pricing/packaging move; 4 = positioning shift / new ICP; 3 = new feature in core wedge; 2 = peripheral feature; 1 = blog/PR; 0 = cosmetic.
Cross-reference signals with your own funnel (PostHog/GA4) — competitor launch + your sign-up dip is a real threat; competitor launch + flat funnel is noise.
Quarterly tam-refresh must compare against last version with explicit delta + driver narrative; refuse to overwrite history.
Tie market signals to GTM action: every severity-≥4 row must produce a "respond / monitor / ignore" decision within one weekly cycle, written into threat-board.md.
Token budget: cap weekly synth at ~30k tokens, monthly strategist at ~80k, quarterly TAM at ~150k; otherwise opus costs balloon.
Scrape politely: rotate UA, respect robots.txt, throttle to 1 req/2s per host, prefer official APIs/RSS where they exist.
Keep a dead-source.md log: when a source goes 30 days without change OR returns errors 3 runs in a row, escalate to human audit.
Versioned market-map.md (one file per quarter) lets the strategist diff Q-over-Q rather than re-derive each time.

rule.statement: Use cheap official APIs and RSS feeds (GitHub releases, public changelogs, RSS) before aggressive crawling of third-party sites like G2, Capterra, LinkedIn, Glassdoor, or Crunchbase.
rule.rationale: Aggressive crawling invites IP blocks, rate-limit bans, and legal letters. Official APIs and RSS provide the same signal with lower legal risk and better reliability.

list.item: claude (Claude Code) — Orchestrate cadence subagents via slash commands (/market-daily, /market-weekly, /market-synth). Docs: docs.anthropic.com/claude/code
gh — Pull competitor GitHub releases / repo activity for OSS competitors. Docs: cli.github.com
tavily API CLI — Agent-native web search, low-noise output. Docs: tavily.com/docs
serpapi / dataforseo — Structured SERP + News results. Docs: serpapi.com / dataforseo.com
playwright / crawl4ai — Render JS-heavy pricing & changelog pages. Docs: playwright.dev / crawl4ai.com
httpx + selectolax — Cheap static-page scraping with HTML diffing. Docs: github.com/projectdiscovery/httpx
feedparser (Python) — RSS/Atom changelog ingestion. Docs: pypi.org/project/feedparser
gh-rss, releases.atom — Built-in RSS for any GitHub repo. Usage: https://github.com/<repo>/releases.atom
crunchbase API — Funding + org data. Docs: data.crunchbase.com/docs
ahrefs / semrush / dataforseo — Keyword + rank monitoring. Docs: ahrefs.com/api / semrush.com/api
Meta Ad Library API — Active competitor ads. Docs: facebook.com/ads/library/api
producthunt API — New entrant detection. Docs: api.producthunt.com
wayback-cdx — Verify historical pricing claims. Docs: archive.org/help/wayback-api
diff-match-patch — Robust block-level diffing for changelog pages. Docs: github.com/google/diff-match-patch
slack webhook / tg-send — Push weekly digest to product/GTM channel. NERO tool: ~/bin/tg-send
cron / systemd-timer / launchd — Schedule cadence agents. Integration: loop skill / schedule skill
Tavily — SaaS, agent-tuned REST API. Cleanest signal for daily market-pulse.
Exa.ai — SaaS, REST API. Semantic search, good for new entrant discovery.
SerpAPI / DataForSEO — SaaS, REST API. Structured Google/Bing/News SERP.
Crunchbase — SaaS, REST API. Funding + org data; rate-limited.
PitchBook — SaaS, limited API. Costly; mostly enterprise.
Similarweb / Semrush / Ahrefs — SaaS, REST API. Traffic + SERP signals.
Sensor Tower / data.ai — SaaS, REST API. Mobile install + revenue estimates.
Meta Ad Library — OSS API. Free, but auth + rate limits.
TikTok Creative Center — SaaS, limited. Web-only; scrape with care.
Klue / Crayon / Kompyte / Contify — SaaS, REST + webhooks. Compete-intel platforms; API surfaces curated alerts.
Visualping / Distill.io / ChangeTower — SaaS, REST + webhooks. Page-change detection if you don't want to host scrapers.
Feedly Leo — SaaS, REST API. AI-curated competitor + market feeds.
Owler — SaaS, REST API. Funding + news aggregation.
Notion / Airtable — SaaS, REST API. Cheap home for market-map.md + threat-board.md.
PostHog — OSS/SaaS, REST API. Cross-reference your funnel against signal events.
Apollo.io / ZoomInfo — SaaS, limited. Headcount/role-change signals; ToS sensitive.
OpenCorporates — OSS API. Public filings, jurisdiction expansions.
Source decay: SaaS competitors disable RSS, hide changelogs behind login, or move to gated Slack — the scan rots silently. Require a quarterly source-health audit.
LLMs hallucinate competitor pricing pages and changelog entries when scrapes fail — every row needs a fetched-at timestamp + raw HTML hash, otherwise a "$49 → $99 price hike" can be fabricated.
Signal volume without prioritization buries real moves under noise (every blog post becomes "competitor activity"). Severity scoring is mandatory.

Scrape ToS / IP risk: aggressive crawling of G2, Capterra, LinkedIn, Glassdoor, Crunchbase invites blocks and legal letters. Use official APIs, RSS, and SerpAPI/Tavily-style aggregators with proper attribution.
Geo blindness: a US-centric scan misses APAC/EU competitors; require segment-tagged source lists.
Pricing pages A/B-test by region/cookie — single-shot scrapes give a misleading reading. Capture multiple geo + logged-out perspectives.
No-news bias: agents anchor on "something must have changed" and fabricate diffs from cosmetic edits (font/CSS). Diff content blocks, not raw HTML.
Without a clear "Outcome" the scan tracks 40 competitors instead of the 5 that move your SOM — agents cheerfully expand scope unless capped.

section.name: Agentic Workflow Pattern
section.p: Wire continuous scanning via Claude Agent SDK with structured outputs (Pydantic/Zod schema enforced). Daily watchers (haiku) fetch, diff, and append to JSONL with atomic locking. Weekly aggregators (sonnet) parse and cluster signal themes. Bi-weekly synthesizers (opus) read summarized weekly reports (not raw haiku dumps) and update versioned market maps. Quarterly strategists (opus) layer the 90-day trend against sales CRM win/loss and produce strategic recommendations. All outputs live under .aidocs/market-intel/. The synthesizer and strategist read prior versions before writing, ensuring no history overwrites — append-only design for audit trail integrity.
section.code: <role>You are {agent}. Continuous market scanning practitioner.</role> <inputs> <since>{ISO8601 last-run timestamp}</since> <competitors>{path to competitor-registry.json}</competitors> <market_map>{path to market-map.md}</market_map> <category_keywords>{list}</category_keywords> </inputs> <rules> - Tag every observation with: source_url, fetched_at, competitor_id, signal_type (changelog|pricing|review|funding|ad|serp|launch), severity (1-5), confidence (0-1). - Reject any change that lacks a source_url + raw-content hash. - Cosmetic-only diffs (CSS, copy reorder) → severity 0, do not surface. - Pricing changes require >=2 geo snapshots before flagging. - For new entrants, score category match (0-1); discard <0.6. - Output JSON matching {schema_path}, plus markdown digest <= 60 lines. </rules> <task>{cadence-specific instruction}</task>

section.name: Competitor Registry and Monitoring Matrix
section.p: Maintain a versioned competitor-registry.json at .aidocs/market-intel/. All additions and removals must go via PR to keep a stable audit trail. Each competitor record includes id (stable key), name, tier (direct|indirect|substitute), changelog_url, pricing_url, review_sources (G2, Capterra, Reddit handles), and geos list. Without stable IDs, daily watchers cannot correlate signals across cadences. The registry is the source of truth for which competitors matter to your strategic Outcome; it is deliberately constrained (5–15 entries, never 40) and reviewed monthly. When a competitor falls below the Outcome threshold, archive it to a dead-competitors list; never delete.

template.purpose: Claude Agent SDK watcher: fetch + diff + hash + append signals.jsonl
Competitor registry schema with id, tier, changelog/pricing URLs, geos, review sources
Crontab entries for all cadence agents

script.purpose: Reject any signal row missing source_url + fetched_at + raw_hash triple
